subject: Biology
title: Plant Life Cycles
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Plant Life Cycles.

hook.text: Some plants live one season and die. Others survive for thousands of years. The strategies differ.

buildup.text: Annuals like wheat complete their life cycle in one year—germinate, flower, seed, die.

discovery.text: Perennials like oak trees live for decades, flowering and seeding many times.

twist.text: Bristlecone pines in California are over 5,000 years old—the oldest known individual organisms.

climax.text: Biennials take two years: grow vegetatively the first, reproduce the second, then die.

punchline.text: One year, two years, or five thousand. Plants play the long game.

quiz.question: How old are the oldest bristlecone pines?
